summary: The child recently completed their rabies vaccinations; thus, they are protected. Administer standard post-exposure treatment consisting of clean wounds care followed by two more doses of the rabies vaccine on day 0 and then again three days later without using human rabies immune globulin due to prior completion of initial vaccines. This approach adheres strictly to guidelines ensuring optimal protection while avoiding over-treatment which may lead to complications. Agent1 reasoned based primarily on maintaining appropriate levels of pre-existing antibodies through timely administration rather than introducing potentially redundant interventions such as HRG despite recent exposures.
